bio.fun_fact: Hobbies include biking, water skiing, fishing, alpine skiing and hiking
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Specialty: Men's Nordic Combined
  • Son of Paul and Karyl Loomis
  • Has one sister, Sam, and one brother, Adam, who is also a member of Team USA
  • Moved to Park City, Utah, at age 15 to find a more adaptive school and higher quality training
  • Named the 2016 USSA Nordic Combined Athlete of the Year
  • Hobbies include biking, water skiing, fishing, alpine skiing and hiking
Biographical Information

Ben Loomis grew up ski jumping at the Flying Eagles Ski Club, following his older brother Adam into the sport at age 5. Nordic combined captured his attention early, and he dedicated himself to mastering both technical precision on the ski jump and the endurance demands of cross-country racing.

At age 15, Loomis made the pivotal decision to move to Park City, Utah, to attend the Winter Sports School, where he could access higher-quality training while balancing his academics. His commitment paid off quickly as he rose through the junior ranks, earning six U.S. Junior National titles and competing at five Junior World Championships between 2014 and 2018. During the 2018 FIS Nordic Junior World Championships, he captured bronze in the 10-kilometer individual Gundersen event and placed fourth in the 5-kilometer event, solidifying his status as one of America's top young Nordic combined athletes. In 2016, he won silver at the Youth Olympic Winter Games Lillehammer 2016 in Nordic combined—the first U.S. medal in the sport at a Youth Games—and was named the USSA Nordic Combined Athlete of the Year.

Loomis made his Olympic debut at age 19 at the Olympic Winter Games Pyeongchang 2018, competing in all three events and gaining invaluable experience on the world's biggest stage. He showed marked improvement by Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022, climbing 26 spots in the normal hill event (finishing 15th), advancing 21 places in the large hill event (19th), and helping Team USA place sixth in the team competition. Between Olympic cycles, Loomis has been a consistent presence on the world cup and world championship circuits, posting his best world cup result—a 12th-place finish at Val di Fiemme during the 2021-22 season—which marked the top world cup finish by a U.S. Nordic combined athlete since 2018. At the 2025 FIS Nordic World Championships, he contributed to strong team performances and walked away from the 2025 U.S. Nordic Combined Summer National Championships with gold and silver medals.

In 2019, Loomis joined the U.S. Army World Class Athlete Program, which supports elite athletes by allowing them to train and compete full time while building military careers. This partnership has enabled him to maintain his focus on international competition while serving his country in a different capacity. Outside of competition, he enjoys biking, water skiing, fishing, alpine skiing and hiking.

Loomis earned his third Olympic nomination for the Olympic Winter Games Milano Cortina 2026, joining teammate Niklas Malacinski for a two-man U.S. Nordic Combined Team.

Olympic Experience
  • 3-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, 7th (Team Sprint Large Hill / 2 x 7.5km - Men), 17th (Gundersen Normal Hill / 10km - Men), 27th (Gundersen Large Hill / 10km - Men)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, 6th (Team Large Hill / 4 x 5km - Men), 15th (Gundersen Normal Hill / 10km - Men), 19th (Gundersen Large Hill / 10km - Men)
    • Olympic Games Pyeongchang 2018, 10th (Team Large Hill / 4 x 5km - Men), 40th (Gundersen Large Hill / 10km - Men), 41th (Gundersen Normal Hill / 10km - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – 8th (Team Large Hill / 4 x 5km - Men), 9th (Team Normal Hill / 2 x 2.5km + 2 x 5km - Mixed), 21th (Compact Normal Hill / 7.5km - Men), 23th (Gundersen Large Hill / 10km - Men)
  • Years of participation: Compact Normal Hill / 7.5km - Men 2025; Gundersen Large Hill / 10km - Men 2019, 2021, 2023, 2025; Team Large Hill / 4 x 5km - Men 2023, 2025; Team Normal Hill / 2 x 2.5km + 2 x 5km - Mixed 2023, 2025; Gundersen Normal Hill / 10km - Men 2019, 2021, 2023; Team Normal Hill / 4 x 5km - Men 2017, 2019, 2021; Team Sprint Large Hill / 2 x 7.5km - Men 2019, 2021
  • Top finish: 7th – 2023 (Team Normal Hill / 2 x 2.5km + 2 x 5km - Mixed)

USA Rugby

Quick Facts
  • In 2005, U.S. Air Force staff sergeant Sebastiana Lopez-Arrellano was riding her motorcycle in Charleston, South Carolina when an animal ran across the road, causing her to swerve into a curb and crash. The impact severed her right leg and ultimately was amputated above the knee. She also had severe hand function trauma in both hands. She went to Walter Reed National Military Medical Center in Bethesda, Maryland and was in a coma for about a month. During her recovery, her recovery care coordinator encouraged her to go to an Air Force Wounded Warrior adaptive sports camp. She found comfort in sports and fitness, earning invitations to the Invictus and Warrior Games where she competed in wheelchair racing, wheelchair rugby, hand cycling, shooting and sitting volleyball
  • Daughter of Felix and Noemi Lopez
  • Has nine siblings, Abraham, Amy, Conrado, Felix, Guadalupe, Josabel, Maria, Veronica and Virginia
  • Has a service dog named Bravo
  • Is first-generation American with her family coming from Mexico
  • Hobbies include cooking, playing music and watching movies.

2017: WCWS All-Tournament Team, NFCA First Team All-American, NFCA First Team All-Southeast Region, All-SEC First Team, SEC Academic Honor Roll

2016: Third Team All-American, First Team All-Southeast Region, NFCA Freshman of the Year, SEC Freshman of the Year, First Team All-SEC, SEC All-Freshman Team, SEC Freshman of the Week (3/28), SEC First Year Academic Honor Roll

PROFESSIONAL HIGHLIGHTS

- Drafted No. 2 overall in the NPF Draft to the USSSA Pride in 2019

GETTING TO KNOW AMANDA LORENZ

Dream Job: To be a college softball coach

Favorite activity: Going to the lake

Favorite type of music: Country because it always gets me in a good mood

Best advice I have ever received: "Only worry about the things you can control" and to just be myself. 

What it means to represent the United States: Representing the USA means everything to me. I have dreamed of wearing Red, White, and Blue and playing for Team USA at the highest level since I was 8 years old. I used to chase down the greats such as Laura Berg, Jennie Finch, Jessica Mendoza, Caitlin Lowe, Natasha Watley, and Lisa Fernandez for their autographs. This is very exciting and unreal opportunity and I am so excited. 

What she loves about playing softball: The friendships that I have formed and the life sessions I have learned. Competing and laughing with my teammates. 

What is your favorite softball memory: Playing in the WCWS

Who were your softball role models growing up? Jennie Finch, Natasha Watley, Jessica Mendoza, Caitlin Lowe, Kelly Kretschman, Lovie Jung

Olympic Experience
  • 5-time Olympian; 1-time Olympic medalist (1 bronze)
    • Olympic Games London 2012, 4th (Individual - Women), 6th (Team - Women)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2008, 5th (Individual - Women)
    • Olympic Games Sydney 2000, 12th (Team - Women), 46th (Individual - Women)
    • Olympic Games Atlanta 1996, 49th (Individual - Women)
    • Olympic Games Barcelona 1992, bronze (Team - Women), 5th (Individual - Women)
Career Highlights

  • 1992 Olympic Games, team bronze
  • 2008 Olympic Games – 5th individual, Closing Ceremony flag bearer for United States
  • 1992, 1996, 2000, 2008, 2012 Olympian
  • 2011 Pan American Games, team silver
  • 2014 Pan American Championships, team gold
  • Multi-time Archery World Cup medalist 
  • United States Archery Team member
  • Multiple USAT Qualifier Series and National event podium placements 

Fun Facts: Khatuna, an accomplished coach in archery, coached actress Jennifer Lawrence for the film The Hunger Games. She was chosen to bear the flag at the Closing Ceremony in the 2008 Games. Khatuna speaks Georgian, Russian, and English, and her favorite television show is Game of Thrones. Khatuna has represented three different nations in the Olympic Games; her son, Levan, is also a competitive recurve archer. Khatuna can quote almost any movie, loves animals and bakes a delicious Georgian baklava.

Quick Facts
  • Brandon is the son of Laura and Steve Loschiavo.
  • He has two older siblings, Amy and Michael.
  • Brandon started diving at age 12 and has also been involved in gymnastics.
  • Says his favorite dives are all of them, although sometimes he likes none of them.
  • Would like to become a diving coach after he is done competing.
  • Enjoys videography, photography, bouldering (rock climbing), backpacking, and camping.
